creator: jah@FreeBSD.org
text: It looks like this was caused by the vfs_unregister_upper() calls I added in 14.  I'm a bit surprised that zfs rollback obliterates the v_mount field of the upper/lower vnodes, but then I'm not familiar with that code.  In any case, unionfs should probably cache the ref'ed upper/lower mount objects returned by vfs_register_upper_from_vp (as nullfs already does), because there are several cases outside of unmount() in which unionfs directly accesses ump->um_[lower|upper]vp->v_mount which aren't likely to be safe in the presence of a concurrent (recursive) forced unmount.
